next_steps: India ink preparation and cryptococcal antigen testing of cerebrospinal fluid
Fungal and mycobacterial cultures of cerebrospinal fluid
Brain MRI with contrast (if compatible with vagus nerve stimulator) or PET-CT scan to evaluate for occult malignancy
Serum and urinary free cortisol and plasma ACTH levels
Empiric antifungal therapy (e.g., liposomal amphotericin B and flucytosine) given the severe hypoglycorrhachia
Start systemic corticosteroids if adrenal insufficiency is ruled out or considered secondary to underlying malignancy, though caution is required given the suspected Cushing's syndrome
